Axon expose **trois surfaces dérivées** (read-only, **non source-of-truth**, reconstructibles à 100% depuis PG) pour permettre aux humains de naviguer la connaissance accumulée sans passer par MCP. Aucune n'est autoritative ; toutes doivent être (re)générables à la demande ET rafraîchies périodiquement pour ne jamais dériver de la vérité PG.

## Trois surfaces canoniques

| Surface | Localisation | Générateur | Canonicité |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Dashboard Elixir/Phoenix** | `src/dashboard/` (Phoenix LiveView, BEAM) | redémarrable via `axonctl` | Non-canonique (observation operator) — cf. CPT-AXO-014 |
| **SOLL autodoc web** | `docs/derived/soll/` (HTML statique par projet) | MCP tool `soll_generate_docs` (`tools_soll.rs`, v3 par projet + root_v2) | Non-canonique (vue dérivée du graphe SOLL) — nouveau CPT à créer |
| **Memgraph projection IST + SOLL** | `/tmp/axon-memgraph-*` + Memgraph Lab cypherl | `scripts/publish-memgraph.sh`, `memgraph_build_cypherl.py`, `memgraph_build_lab_collection.py` | Non-canonique (graphe visuel humain) — cf. CPT-AXO-015 |

## Règles opérationnelles

- **Régénérables** : toute surface peut être recompilée à partir de l'état PG courant. Aucune ne contient de donnée originale.
- **Non-autoritative** : un LLM n'utilise JAMAIS ces surfaces comme source de vérité. La vérité reste PG (lue via MCP). Ces surfaces sont pour humains.
- **Incrémentale quand possible** : SOLL autodoc utilise un manifeste `_manifest.json` avec `pages[].content_hash` + `deleted_paths` pour ne réécrire que les pages dont le hash a changé. Memgraph projection à rendre incrémentale sur le même modèle (REQ futur).
- **Cadence de rafraîchissement** : aujourd'hui **à la demande uniquement** (gap connu). Cible : autodoc régénérée post-mutation SOLL OU au moins 1×/heure ; Memgraph rafraîchi post-promote-live OU 1×/jour.
- **Échec gracieux** : la défaillance d'une surface de visualisation ne doit JAMAIS bloquer le runtime canonique (Brain, Indexer). Best-effort, fire-and-forget.

## Acceptance

- Un nouveau membre de l'équipe peut, sans MCP, comprendre l'architecture en parcourant le dashboard + l'autodoc + Memgraph.
- Aucun LLM n'écrit « je consulte le dashboard pour connaître l'état » — il consulte MCP.
- Une dérive entre une surface et PG est détectable et corrigeable par régénération complete ou diff incrémental.
- Le 3-surfaces forment une **façade humaine** uniforme : naviguer le savoir Axon sans côté commande.

## Rationale (terse)

Sans cette pillar : les trois surfaces flottent comme accessoires implémentation. Avec : elles deviennent un sous-produit explicite d'Axon, garantissant que l'investissement dans le knowledge graph est consommable par humains autant que par LLMs (cf. VIS-AXO-001 « accéder instantanément et actionnablement »).

La séparation entre ce pilier (visualisation humaine non-canonique) et PIL-AXO-002 (surface MCP machine-stable canonique) est explicite : les deux sont des surfaces de consommation, mais l'une est autoritative et l'autre est dérivée.

## Comportement attendu

**Règle universelle** pour Local Graph (pages de nœuds) ET Hierarchy Focus (pages d'index) :
- **Niveau 0** = nœud courant. Position : **centre**.
- **Niveau +1** = ancetres directs (plus macro). Position : **gauche**.
- **Niveau -1** = descendants directs (plus micro). Position : **droite**.
- **Profondeur maximale = ±1**. Jamais grand-parents ni petits-enfants. Sinon perte de lisibilité (citation opérateur).

## Problème

Les 3 panneaux de chaque page-nœud autodoc (`<PROJECT>/nodes/<ID>.html` générée par `soll_generate_docs_v3`, cf. CPT-AXO-055) doivent pouvoir être individuellement masqués, mais le comportement actuel des boutons est asymétrique et casse l'équilibre du layout.

**Layout initial (aucun toggle)** :
```
[ Project Tree (↑300px) | Local Graph (flex, large) | Detail (↑360px) ]
```

**Comportement actuel observé (session 23, 2026-05-13)** :

| Action | Comportement actuel | Comportement attendu |
|---|---|---|
| Toggle Detail | Detail disparaît, Local Graph s'étend pour reprendre la place de Detail. Project Tree reste à sa largeur initiale. **CORRECT.** | Identique — déjà ce qu'il faut. |
| Toggle Tree | Project Tree disparaît mais le layout casse : Detail s'étend pleine page, Local Graph descend en-dessous (rupture flex → stack vertical). **CASSÉ.** | Project Tree disparaît, Local Graph s'étend pour reprendre la place de Tree. Detail reste à sa largeur initiale. |
| Toggle Tree + Toggle Detail (les deux activés) | Non testé ; présumablement cassé. | Local Graph occupe TOUTE la largeur (pleine page). |
| **Toggle Graph (bouton à créer)** | Bouton inexistant. | Local Graph disparaît, Project Tree et Detail partagent la largeur 50/50. |

## Règle universelle proposée

Quand un panneau est masqué, sa largeur est réabsorbée par les panneaux restants selon les règles :

1. **Si Local Graph (centre) est visible** : il absorbe TOUT l'espace libéré par les côtés masqués. Les côtés qui restent visibles gardent leur largeur d'origine.
2. **Si Local Graph est masqué** (via le nouveau Toggle Graph) : les panneaux Tree + Detail visibles partagent l'espace également (50/50 si les deux sont là).

## Comportement attendu pour les 8 combinaisons de toggles

| Tree | Graph | Detail | Layout résultant |
|---|---|---|---|
| visible | visible | visible | Tree 300 / Graph flex / Detail 360 (initial) |
| visible | visible | masqué | Tree 300 / Graph flex (absorbe Detail) |
| masqué | visible | visible | Graph flex (absorbe Tree) / Detail 360 |
| masqué | visible | masqué | Graph pleine largeur |
| visible | masqué | visible | Tree 50% / Detail 50% |
| visible | masqué | masqué | Tree pleine largeur |
| masqué | masqué | visible | Detail pleine largeur |
| masqué | masqué | masqué | (à décider : désactiver le 3ème toggle quand 2 sont déjà masqués OU afficher un message "empty layout") |

## Pattern

```
XXX-YYY-NNN
```

## Composition

| Segment | Position | Cardinalité | Classe | Exemples valides | Exemples invalides |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| `XXX` | 1-3 | 3 chars (fixe) | `[A-Z]{3}` | `REQ`, `CPT`, `DEC`, `MIL`, `VAL`, `STK`, `GUI`, `PIL`, `VIS`, `REV`, `PRV` | `REQU` (4), `Req` (lower), `R3Q` (digit) |
| `-` | 4 | 1 char | littéral `-` | | |
| `YYY` | 5-7 | 3 chars (fixe) | `[A-Z][A-Z0-9]{2}` (1ère lettre, 2 alphanum, majuscule) | `AXO`, `BKS`, `PRO`, `TE2`, `A12`, `BX9` | `1AX` (1ère digit), `axo` (lower), `AX` (2 chars), `AXON` (4 chars) |
| `-` | 8 | 1 char | littéral `-` | | |
| `NNN` | 9+ | min 3 chars, pas de cap | `[0-9]{3,}` zero-padded à gauche pour atteindre min 3 chiffres | `001`, `042`, `100`, `999`, `1000`, `99999`, `1000000` | `1` (<3), `01a` (lettre), `00` (<3) |

## Regex canonique

```
^[A-Z]{3}-[A-Z][A-Z0-9]{2}-[0-9]{3,}$
```

## Règles de numérotation

1. **Démarrage** : `NNN` commence à `1` → affiché `001`.
2. **Incrément** : +1 par allocation. `001` → `002` → … → `099` → `100` → … → `999` → `1000` → … (pas de cap supérieur).
3. **Zero-padding** : appliqué jusqu'à atteindre 3 chiffres min, puis longueur naturelle.
4. **Compteur par couple `(XXX, YYY)`** : `REQ-AXO-NNN` et `REQ-BKS-NNN` ont séquences indépendantes ; idem `CPT-AXO-NNN` vs `REQ-AXO-NNN`.
5. **Source canonique** : `soll.Registry.last_{type}` par row `project_code`. L'allocateur ne chaîne JAMAIS sur `MAX(soll.Node.id)` — ce path a polluer le compteur (incident session-23 REQ-AXO-90007).
6. **Unicité** : ID complet = PRIMARY KEY de `soll.Node`.

## Génération Rust

```rust
format!("{xxx}-{yyy}-{nnn:03}", xxx = prefix, yyy = project_code, nnn = next)
```

Le spécificateur `{:03}` garantit zero-padding min 3 chiffres, longueur naturelle au-delà.

## Application DDL (defense-in-depth, idempotente)

`db/ddl/01_soll_schema.sql` :
- `CHECK (id ~ '^[A-Z]{3}-[A-Z][A-Z0-9]{2}-[0-9]{3,}$')` sur `soll.Node.id`
- `CHECK (project_code ~ '^[A-Z][A-Z0-9]{2}$')` sur project_code dans Node, Edge, Revision, RevisionChange, RevisionPreview, McpJob
- Mode `NOT VALID` au déploiement pour préserver rows légacy. Futures écritures DOIVENT conformer.
- Après cleanup légacy : `ALTER TABLE ... VALIDATE CONSTRAINT ...` pour durcir.

## Dérogations

Aucune. Si un cas semble nécessiter une exception, utiliser un nouveau type d'entité 3-lettres (extension du jeu `XXX`) plutôt qu'étendre le format.

## Détection violation

- Allocateur sortant un suffix != Registry+1 → incident axon-bug.
- INSERT manuel non-conforme → CHECK constraint reject.
- project_code != 3 chars / 1ère digit / lowercase → CHECK constraint reject.

Copy-paste bug découvert 2026-05-14. `pipeline_v2/channels.rs::B3_BATCH_TIMEOUT_MS_DEFAULT = 10` était copié de A3 (qui a ce floor sous directive opérateur explicite 2026-05-12 'envoyer ce qu'on a toutes les 10 ms' pour latence FTS). MAIS B3 est le terminal vector writer — latence aval = zéro.

Effet : avec B2 arrivant à 100-300/s et B3 tick toutes les 10ms, le buffer B3 contenait ~1-3 rows par tick avant flush → batch_size=64 jamais atteint → effective B3 = single-row HNSW UPSERT en boucle → exactement la contention que multi-row UPSERT était censé amortir.

Fix :
- B3_BATCH_SIZE_DEFAULT : 64 → 256 (4× B2 pour drainer plus vite que B2 produit)
- B3_BATCH_TIMEOUT_MS_DEFAULT : 10 → 200ms (match B2 cadence)

Gain attendu : B3 effective à 200-500 emb/s (pgvector HNSW batched rate) au lieu de 30-50 emb/s (single-row insert rate). Mesure réelle confounded par option-b token bucket bench en parallèle — voir DEC-AXO-089.

**Évidence** : commit 07f0e4bf, push origin/main.

## Historical content (DuckDB era, pre-2026-05-09)

### Purpose
The `writer_ctx: Mutex<*mut c_void>` field in `src/axon-core/src/graph.rs:33` serializes ALL DuckDB writes through one process-wide mutex. Every Rust caller that mutates the canonical IST must acquire this lock first.

### Shape (DuckDB-era)
- Single `Mutex<*mut c_void>` wrapping the DuckDB connection pointer (FFI handle from axon-plugin-duckdb)
- Acquirers: Writer Actor (`worker.rs::spawn_writer_actor`), vector_lane (`embedder/vector_worker_loop.rs`), and any direct `graph_store.*` write call site
- Lock held for entire BEGIN/COMMIT span on batch writes — no fine-grained per-statement locking
- No reader/writer split: read paths take the same mutex (DuckDB connection is not Send+Sync at the API surface used)
- Invariant: only one writer makes progress at a time; vector_lane chunk writes contend directly with Writer Actor file/symbol/edge batches

## Three structural faults (code verified)

### Fault 1: `soll.node` PRIMARY KEY = `(id)` not `(id, project_code)`
Source : `db/ddl/*.sql` + `src/axon-core/src/postgres/ddl.rs`. ID uniqueness is GLOBAL across projects. A project that mis-uses an ID overwrites another project's node. ID format CHECK `^[A-Z]{3}-[A-Z][A-Z0-9]{2}-[0-9]{3,}$` includes project_code, but the unique index does not enforce per-project scope.

### Fault 2: `soll_manager.create` is a silent UPSERT
Source : `src/axon-core/src/mcp/tools_soll/manager.rs:333` — the create path executes:
```
INSERT INTO soll.Node (id, type, project_code, title, description, status, metadata)
VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)
ON CONFLICT (id) DO UPDATE SET
  project_code = EXCLUDED.project_code,
  title        = EXCLUDED.title,
  description  = EXCLUDED.description,
  status       = EXCLUDED.status,
  metadata     = EXCLUDED.metadata
```
A `create` action MUST NOT overwrite. Should be `ON CONFLICT (id) DO NOTHING` returning an explicit error to the caller (pattern is already used in `src/axon-core/src/postgres/seed.rs:260`). Currently `create` and `update` are functionally identical — confusing contract and unsafe.

### Fault 3: counter not seeded when projectcoderegistry INSERT bypassed normal init path
Source : `src/axon-core/src/mcp/tools_soll/project_registry.rs:36` — the project init flow inserts a `soll.Registry` row with all `last_*` defaulting to 0. There is no DB trigger that auto-seeds `last_*` from `MAX(numeric_suffix)` per type when a `projectcoderegistry` row is added later. A direct DB INSERT into `projectcoderegistry` (necessary as workaround for PRO not being registered via normal path) creates the registry counter row stuck at 0 — any subsequent `create` collides with existing IDs.

## Combined incident (2026-05-14)
1. `projectcoderegistry` had PRO data (30+ nodes) but missing registry row → `soll_manager.create project_code=PRO` rejected.
2. Direct SQL INSERT into `projectcoderegistry` (operator-authorized) added PRO, with `last_*=0` defaults.
3. `soll_manager.create entity=guideline project_code=PRO` allocated `GUI-PRO-001` (counter 0→1).
4. Fault 2 triggered : silent overwrite of existing `GUI-PRO-001 "TDD Obligatoire"`.
5. Fault 1 enabled the cross-project collision in the first place.
6. No `soll.revisionchange` row written → no rollback via SOLL natives.
7. Recovery via external SOLL_EXPORT_2026-05-08 file (GUI exact) + cross-file references (DEC reconstructed).

## Required fixes
1. Patch `manager.rs:333` → `ON CONFLICT (id) DO NOTHING` + return error envelope `{"error":"id_exists","id":"<X>","hint":"use action=update instead"}`.
2. Patch `manager.rs` create path to ALSO emit `soll.revisionchange(action='create', before_json=NULL, after_json=<row>)` for every successful create. Same for update path.
3. Patch `db/ddl/*.sql` to change `soll.node` PK from `(id)` to `(id, project_code)`. Coordinate with all FK references to `soll.node(id)` (likely soll.edge, soll.revisionchange).
4. Add DB trigger on `INSERT OR UPDATE` of `soll.projectcoderegistry` that seeds `soll.registry.last_*` from `SELECT MAX(numeric_suffix) FROM soll.node WHERE project_code=NEW.project_code GROUP BY type` per type.

## Acceptance
1. Test : `soll_manager.create` with existing ID → fails with explicit error, no overwrite.
2. Test : every `soll_manager.create/update` emits `soll.revisionchange` row.
3. Test : INSERT into `projectcoderegistry` for project P with existing P-scope nodes auto-seeds counters.
4. Test : two projects cannot collide on the same canonical ID NNN (`(id, project_code)` PK enforces).

## ROOT CAUSE du hang `soll_apply_plan` (decouverte 2026-05-10 session 11)

**FFI connection-pinning bug** déjà documenté dans `execute_batch` (graph_query.rs:822-832, REQ-AXO-244 fix joint multi-statements en single execute) mais NON corrigé pour `axon_soll_commit_revision` qui fait des `BEGIN/INSERT/UPDATE/COMMIT` séparés en multiple `pg_execute` calls.

Chaque `pg_execute` récupère une connexion FRESH du deadpool. Sequence:
1. Connection A : `BEGIN TRANSACTION` (workflow_plan.rs:163)
2. Connection B : `INSERT INTO soll.Revision ...` (workflow_plan.rs:165) — nouvelle conn, INSERT autocommit
3. Connection C : `INSERT INTO soll.RevisionChange ...` (apply_operation_with_audit) — nouvelle conn, autocommit
4. Connection D : `INSERT INTO soll.Node ...` (axon_soll_manager) — nouvelle conn
5. Connection E : `SELECT FROM soll.Traceability ...` (snapshot_entity / broken_file_evidence)
6. ...

**Connection A reste 'idle in transaction' indefiniment**, holding row locks accumulés pendant son BEGIN. Concrete observation 2026-05-10 00:01:15 UTC:

```
blocked_pid = 4372  INSERT INTO soll.ProjectCodeRegistry ('APS', 'aps3d', ...)
blocking_pid = 4373 (idle in transaction) SELECT FROM soll.Traceability WHERE soll_entity_id = 'REQ-AXO-254'
```

pid 4373 holds `RowExclusiveLock` sur `soll.projectcoderegistry` (UPSERT précédent dans la transaction ouverte), pid 4372 wait `ShareLock on transactionid` 4373.

Quand l'opérateur fait stop --hard, conn 4373 EOF, libere les locks, mais le brain crash log dans le tmux. Reproduit deterministiquement.

1. Cross-project IST queries triviales (1 SELECT avec project_code IN (...))
2. Best practice propagation native (axon_apply_guidelines GUI-PRO-001 → enfants)

6. Foreign keys cross-project possibles (REQ-AXO-066 BELONGS_TO PIL-PRO-001)
7. Operational simplicity (1 DB à backuper, 1 connexion)

## Diagnosis (operator framework, REQ-AXO-252)

**Binding constraint: GPU per-iteration dispatch overhead**, NOT producer, NOT writer.

Proof:
- Producer ceiling (Bench 1 single thread): 87 ch/s. Bench 2b queue_high_water=63/64 (full) confirms 4 producers can saturate GPU input.
- Writer harness (Bench 3 noop): 218,738 ch/s. Six orders of magnitude above any realistic GPU rate — not the bottleneck on this side.
- GPU **peak in-flight** (Bench 2 p50): **268-272 ch/s** — this is the GPU's true compute capacity per batch.
- GPU **sustained wall-time** (Bench 2 mean): **72-77 ch/s** — wall clock includes ~75% non-iteration overhead.
- Gap = ~200 ch/s lost **between** iterations (after warmup, after model+TRT loaded).

Likely sources of the inter-iteration overhead (need profiling to confirm):
1. ORT/TRT session state reset between embed calls
2. CUDA stream sync at batch boundaries
3. Token preparation overhead not accounted for in `tokenize_ms` field
4. Vec<String> → fastembed marshalling cost

## Implications for REQ-AXO-252 north-star ≥150 ch/s

- VAL-AXO-050 (prior probe) reported 105 ch/s @ batch=128 mean. This campaign reports 72.53 ch/s under similar conditions — ~30% drift. Possible causes: machine state, ORT/TRT artifact version (since we updated REQ-AXO-247-254 PG path commits), model snapshot. Not investigated this campaign.
- The GPU has ×3.7 headroom (270 peak) — a redesign of the embed loop to eliminate inter-iteration overhead would unlock the throughput north-star.
- Operator ideas to test next:
  - Pre-allocate fixed input/output buffers (reuse across iterations)
  - Pin GPU memory for input copies
  - Use ORT `IoBinding` instead of allocating new tensors per call
  - Multi-stream concurrent embed (subject to VRAM)

## Status (2026-05-03 20:30Z)
**Layer 1 — dispatch wiring**: FIXED in commit `6cfdbbe` (live build v0.8.0-162-ge68e459). `runtime_boot::run_indexer` and `run_brain` now short-circuit into `embedder::run_gpu_embed_subprocess_stdio()` when `AXON_GPU_EMBED_SERVICE_CHILD=1`, before tokio runtime / boot init. **Layer 2 — ORT dylib dlopen**: NEW BUG, tracked separately as REQ-AXO-173 (P=high). Subprocess now panics on `OrtGpuFirstTextEmbedding::try_new` because LD_LIBRARY_PATH lacks the nix-store onnxruntime-1.24.4 path. End-to-end Pipeline 2 still broken until 173 lands.

## Root cause of the original symptom (verified 2026-05-03)
The parent fork-execs with `AXON_GPU_EMBED_SERVICE_CHILD=1` (gpu_backend.rs:380). The child handler `run_gpu_embed_subprocess_stdio()` exists at embedder.rs:3900 and writes the expected `GpuEmbedSubprocessInit` JSON. But no code path read the env var — `run_indexer` / `run_brain` jumped straight into tokio + full boot. The child ran the normal indexer init, conflicted with the parent's locks/ports, never wrote the JSON handshake, parent read stdout indefinitely, child eventually exited → zombie. 9-14 zombies stacked because each batch retried via `recycle()` (gpu_backend.rs:430).

- Added `gpu_embed_subprocess_child_requested()` predicate (canonical: only literal `"1"`)
- Short-circuit at top of `run_indexer()` and `run_brain()` → call `run_gpu_embed_subprocess_stdio()` and return, before tokio/boot
- Unit test `gpu_embed_subprocess_child_requested_reads_canonical_env` covers unset / `=1` / `=0` / `=true` cases

Plain `./scripts/axon --instance live start` currently defaults to `--indexer-graph`, regardless of what role was running before the runtime went down.

Observed 2026-05-03: Live had been running brain (per the 2026-05-03 handoff smoke-test contract `Runtime identity: axon-live-axon-brain`). After a stale PID + WSL restart, plain `start` silently switched the live runtime to `indexer-graph` mode — producing a HEALTHY indexer with no MCP socket. The customer-facing `axon init` workflow then couldn't proceed.

This is a deliverability gap: the script's default contradicts the customer-facing contract for the same instance. Two complementary fixes:
1. Persist the last successfully started role in `.axon/instance-state.json` (per instance kind). Plain `start` resumes it. Mode change requires an explicit flag.
2. When no prior role exists, default to `--brain-only` (the customer-facing role; indexers are an explicit operational choice).

Acceptance criteria:

Observed 2026-05-03 in live IST `main.File` table: 1 row with concatenated string values across THREE columns:
  - path: `src/axon-core/src/mcp/tools_framework_path.rs/home/dstadel/projects/axon/src/axon-core/src/mcp/tools_framework_path.rs` (path doubled)
  - file_stage: `promotedgraph_indexed` (concat of `promoted` + `graph_indexed`)
  - status: `pendingindexed` (concat of `pending` + `indexed`)
  - status_reason: `requeued_after_writer_batch_failureindexed_success_full` (also concatenated)

This is a race condition on the File-lifecycle writer where two transitions wrote string values that got APPENDED (string concatenation) instead of REPLACED. Same family as REQ-AXO-097/099 (env-var bus race) but on the IST File writer path.

The single corrupt row is a low-impact data quality issue (1 of 14k+ files), but the underlying bug pattern can re-trigger and produce more corrupt rows under load. It also poisons any pipeline-1 audit aggregating file_stage counts.

Fix:
1. Identify the writer code path that performed the racing UPDATEs. Likely candidates: graph_ingestion.rs File status updates, FileLifecycleEvent emitter.
2. Wrap multi-column UPDATEs in a transaction OR use a single REPLACE statement.
3. Add a soft-validation check in `soll_validate` (or new `runtime_filesystem_health` REQ-AXO-165) that flags rows whose path/stage/status do not match canonical enum values.
4. Cleanup script: `UPDATE main.File SET file_stage='graph_indexed', status='indexed', status_reason='indexed_success_full', path='src/axon-core/src/mcp/tools_framework_path.rs' WHERE path LIKE '%tools_framework_path.rs%tools_framework_path.rs'`.

Acceptance criteria:
1. Race-source identified and fixed (transactional update).
2. SOLL self-validate flags concat-corrupt rows (file_stage NOT IN known enum, or path containing repeat substring).
3. The 1 currently corrupt row is fixed by manual UPDATE or migration.

## Friction observed (CPT-AXO-025 branch 2 / 3)

Session 2026-05-06 ~12:30 UTC committed two consecutive operations:
- `c3c79d2` docs(working-notes): handoff for async writer redesign (REQ-AXO-193 direction E) -- 184 LOC markdown
- `0419197` docs: drop markdown handoff -- canonical content lives in REQ-AXO-193 (SOLL)

The deletion commit's claim ('canonical content lives in REQ-AXO-193') was FALSE at deletion time. REQ-AXO-193's SOLL description contained directions A-D only; direction E (the operator-confirmed selection, plus E.1->E.6 phased plan with LOC estimates, anti-patterns, pre-flight checklist, risks table, live runtime contract) was never written into SOLL before the markdown was deleted.

Next-session bootstrap (this session) recovered the markdown via `git show c3c79d2:...` and re-attached the content to REQ-AXO-193 via soll_manager(action=update). Recovery cost: ~5 minutes of session budget, plus the risk that the deletion commit could have been GC'd or amended away.

## Why this is an Axon-product issue (not pilot error)

The protocol CPT-AXO-019 'documente' specifies SOLL as the canonical home for plans/handoffs. It does NOT specify that the writer must verify the write before deleting the source. CPT-AXO-024 'LLM-only doc methodology' similarly trusts the writer.

The LLM committing the deletion (Claude Opus 4.7 V3, prior session) had no mechanism to detect that the soll_manager(update) call it made earlier was either (a) never made, (b) made against a stale revision, or (c) silently dropped fields when the description got too long. Result: the assertion in the commit message was ungrounded.

## Customer-value framing

Any LLM agent equipped with Axon will eventually make the same class of mistake: write to SOLL, then delete the staging artifact, without verifying SOLL absorbed the content correctly. For a customer team using Axon for institutional memory, this manifests as 'I asked the agent to file the handoff and it confirmed it did, but the content is missing from SOLL'. Trust in the canonical store erodes silently.

CPT-AXO-018 (MCP contract hygiene) already requires every public MCP tool to return machine-actionable output. soll_manager update returns 'Update succeeded for `<ID>`' but does NOT return a content hash or revision id that the caller can verify against the input. A machine-verifiable round-trip is missing.

## Proposed remediations (non-binding, pick one or combine)

1. **Tool-level**: soll_manager update returns `{revision_id, content_hash, length_bytes}` so the caller can hash the input and assert match before deleting siblings.
2. **Workflow-level**: extend CPT-AXO-019 to require: write -> immediate read-back via cypher -> substring assertion -> THEN delete sibling. Codify as guideline.
3. **Pre-flight check**: extend `axon_pre_flight_check` to detect commits that delete files containing the substring 'handoff' or 'plan' AND reference a SOLL ID in the deletion message, and verify the referenced SOLL node was updated within the same N-commit window. Block delivery if not.
4. **Lint-style**: a new MCP tool `soll_verify_canonical_transfer(soll_id, source_text)` that returns boolean + diff.
